title: Show interval due dates on reminders (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month)

requirements.text: Update the backend spaced repetition intervals from the current set (1-day, 3-day, 30-day, 3-month) to (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month). Update all interval label strings, scheduling logic, and any hardcoded interval arrays in backend/main.mo to reflect the five intervals: '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month'.
acceptanceCriteria: When a topic is added, exactly five reminder entries are created with intervals: 1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month.
getDueReminders returns reminders with interval labels '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month'.
markReminderDone accepts '7-day' and '1-month' as valid interval values.
The old '30-day' interval label is no longer used anywhere in the backend.

requirements.text: Update the DueReminders frontend component to display the due date (or time remaining) alongside each reminder's interval badge. For each due reminder item, show a human-readable label indicating when it is due (e.g., 'Due today', 'Due in 2 days', 'Overdue by 1 day'). Also update the interval badge labels to match the new set: '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month'.

quotes: show four intervals but not showing when they are

acceptanceCriteria: Each due reminder card shows the interval label (e.g., '7-day', '1-month') as a badge.
Each due reminder card shows a human-readable due date or relative time string (e.g., 'Due today', 'Overdue by 1 day', 'Due in 3 days').
The five intervals '1-day', '3-day', '7-day', '1-month', '3-month' are all represented correctly in the UI.
The old '30-day' label no longer appears anywhere in the frontend.

requirements.text: Update the TopicManager component and any other frontend section that lists or references reminder intervals to display all five intervals (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month) with their scheduled due dates visible. If the topic list shows upcoming reminder dates per topic, ensure all five interval entries are rendered with a formatted date string.

quotes: show four intervals but not showing when they are

acceptanceCriteria: Any place in the UI that lists intervals shows all five: 1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month.
Scheduled revision dates are displayed in a readable format (e.g., 'Jan 15, 2025') wherever intervals are shown.
No references to the old '30-day' interval remain in the frontend.

constraints: Do not change the backend API surface (addTopic, getTopics, getDueReminders, markReminderDone, getStudyProgress remain as-is).
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
nonGoals: Adding new reminder intervals beyond the five specified (1-day, 3-day, 7-day, 1-month, 3-month).
Changing the overall visual theme or layout beyond what is needed to display due date information.
Adding real-time push notifications or email reminders.
